1.solution:
Statement I: 900/(n‚ąí10) ‚ąí 900/n = 15 ‚Üí n¬≤‚ąí10n‚ąí600=0 ‚Üí n=30 (unique). Sufficient.
Statement II: n‚Č•25 and 900/n‚Č§45 ‚Üí n‚Č•20, n‚Č•25. Multiple values possible. Not sufficient.
Answer: A (I alone).

2.solution:
Total distance = 38√ó2+48√ó3 = 220 km. Deepanshi drives equal thirds = 220/3 km at 35, 45, 55 km/h.
Mileage: 35‚Üí20, 45‚Üí28, 55‚Üí20 km/L. Diesel = (220/3)(1/20+1/28+1/20) = (220/3)(19/140) = 4180/420 ‚Čą 9.952 ‚Čą 9.9 L.
Answer: C (9.9 litres).

3.solution:
To minimize diesel over 220 km, maximize mileage ‚Üí drive at 45 km/h (28 km/L).
‚Üí Diesel = 220/28 = 55/7 ‚Čą 7.857 ‚Čą 7.85 L.
Answer: A (7.85 litres).

4.solution:
(a+5)/(b+5)<a/b ‚ÜĒ b<a. Stmt I: a=56<b=81 ‚Üí no. Stmt II: avg<b ‚Üí a<b ‚Üí no (answers it). Stmt III: a>b ‚Üí yes.
Each statement alone answers the question. Any one is sufficient.
Answer: D (Any one of them).

6.solution:
F+2V=4; F+4V=6.5. ‚Üí 2V=2.5 ‚Üí V=1.25, F=1.5.
‚Üí Charges for 5 hours = 1.5+5√ó1.25 = 7.75 lakhs.
Answer: B (‚āĻ7.75 Lakh).

7.solution:
h = k‚ąöage. At age 9: 15=3k ‚Üí k=5.
‚Üí At age 49: h=5√ó‚ąö49=5√ó7=35 feet.
Answer: C (35 feet).

10.solution:
Elk:Deer=5:6, antler:no-antler=7:3. Deer without antler=210. With 3/4 of deer having antlers: k=140.
‚Üí Elk=700, deer with antler=630, elk with antler=448. No-antler total=462; elk without=252; deer without=210 ‚úď.
‚Üí Difference=448‚ąí210=238.
Answer: A (238).
